events.title: Ganesh Chaturthi
events.description: Ganesh Chaturthi — the grand arrival of Lord Ganesha — celebrated with elaborate pandal installations, aarti, modak offerings, devotional music, and ten days of the most joyful and colorful religious festivities in Maharashtra and across India.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Ganesh Chaturthi is one of India's grandest religious festivals — genZScore 5-7. The first day — the arrival of Ganesha — is the most elaborately celebrated, with the entire community dressed in their finest ethnic wear to welcome the deity. The fashion energy is joyful, vibrant, and devotional simultaneously. Red and yellow are Lord Ganesha's most sacred colors — these are the dominant catalog-relevant palette. Saffron, orange, and gold are equally auspicious. The festival spans ten days which means multiple outfit moments — Day 1 is the grandest. Maharashtra connection means the Nauvari saree (nine-yard saree) is uniquely relevant for women — the traditional Maharashtrian draping style. Men in dhoti-kurta or kurta-pyjama. September in Bengaluru is late monsoon — breathable fabrics are essential for outdoor pandal visits. The pandal-hopping culture means comfortable yet festive ethnic dressing. GenZ crowd in Maharashtra and Bengaluru's Maharashtrian community dresses with maximum ethnic pride. Jewellery is traditional and gold-heavy. Kids ethnic sets are extremely relevant — Ganesh Chaturthi is a deeply family festival.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Ganesh Chaturthi Day 1 is the grandest ethnic fashion moment of the festival — welcome Lord Ganesha in your most vibrant and auspicious ethnic look. Red and yellow are the most sacred colors — a red silk-feel saree with gold zari border or a yellow ethnic set with gold embroidery are the most catalog-relevant choices. The Nauvari saree — Maharashtra's iconic nine-yard saree in traditional draping — is uniquely relevant for the Maharashtrian community and highly shoppable. Paithani-inspired weaves with peacock and lotus motifs are deeply appropriate for the festival's cultural context. Women in vibrant ethnic sets with gota-patti or zari detailing, or a colorful lehenga choli with mirror-work for the evening aarti. Men in a traditional dhoti-kurta in white or cream with a colored dupatta, or a festive kurta-pyjama in red, saffron, or yellow. A waistcoat over the kurta elevates the men's Ganesh Chaturthi look beautifully. Pandal-hopping means comfortable yet festive dressing — breathable cotton-silk blends handle the September monsoon humidity beautifully. Gold temple jewellery is the hero accessory — layered necklaces, heavy jhumkas, stacked gold bangles, kamarbandh, and maang tikka. Kids ethnic sets in red and yellow are the most heartwarming catalog category — little ones dressed for Ganpati's arrival. The overall energy is — dressed in Ganesha's most auspicious colors, welcoming the remover of obstacles with maximum devotion and maximum style.

app.headline: Ganpati Bappa Morya. Dress Divinely.
lines: Red saree, yellow ethnic set, gold temple jewels — Ganesh Chaturthi sorted.
Paithani weaves and gold borders for Bappa's grand arrival.
Dress in Ganesha's colors — red, yellow, and all the gold.

social_media.headline: Ganpati Bappa. Festive Fits. Day 1.
lines: POV: wore a red silk saree with gold temple jewellery to Ganesh Chaturthi and Bappa arrived in style.
Ganesh Chaturthi Day 1 fit check — vibrant ethnic wear, gold temple jewels, paithani weaves, dressed for the most joyful arrival of the year. Ganpati Bappa Morya. 🐘

events.title: World Tourism Day Pop-ups
events.description: World Tourism Day pop-ups take over Bengaluru — city-wide cultural showcases, travel photography exhibitions, street food from global cuisines, local heritage walks, and the kind of wanderlust-fueled community energy that makes you want to pack a bag immediately.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: World Tourism Day pop-ups are a GenZ lifestyle and travel culture moment — genZScore 6-8. The crowd is travel-obsessed, aesthetically aware, and social-media-native GenZ who treat travel events like fashion opportunities — everything is content. The fashion energy is travel blogger meets global citizen meets Bengaluru indie cool. September 27th in Bengaluru is the tail end of monsoon transitioning toward the pleasant October season — slightly cooler than peak monsoon, more comfortable for outdoor pop-ups. The travel aesthetic is the brief — versatile, layered, interesting, and globally referential. Think travel blogger OOTD — linen shirts, utility co-ords, interesting prints from global textile traditions, comfortable silhouettes for walking, and the kind of outfit that looks equally good in a Bengaluru pop-up and a European street. The event's city-wide nature means outdoor walking between venues — practical yet stylish. Tote bags and crossbody bags are essential. Caps for September sun. The global textile reference is uniquely strong — ikat, block prints, global pattern references, and interesting textures that reference world cultures are deeply on-brand.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: World Tourism Day pop-ups are the travel blogger aesthetic at its most authentic — comfortable, globally referential, and always content-ready. The event's city-wide walking nature means practical silhouettes that look incredible in photos — above-knee hemlines, comfortable waistbands, and breathable fabrics are non-negotiable. Lead with globally-inspired textile prints — ikat co-ords, block-print shirts, kalamkari dresses, and ajrak-inspired patterns are deeply on-brand for a travel culture event. Women in a relaxed ikat co-ord, a flowy block-print dress in earthy tones, or a linen utility set with interesting textile details. Men in a camp collar shirt in a global print — ikat, block-print, or batik-inspired — with straight-fit linen trousers or cargo shorts. The cargo detail is uniquely relevant for a walking pop-up event — functional pockets for maps, phones, and snacks. A lightweight linen blazer adds a global-traveller sophistication layer. Tote bags are the hero accessory — canvas or printed totes are both functional for carrying pop-up purchases AND look great in travel content. A crossbody or sling bag as an alternative. Caps in neutral earthy tones for the September sun. Watches are relevant — the traveller always knows the time. Wallets for the day of exploring and purchasing. Interesting artisanal jewellery — small hoop earrings, a simple layered necklace, one statement ring. Mobile accessories for the inevitable content creation. The overall energy is — dressed like your next destination is already booked and today's pop-up is the preview.

app.headline: World Tourism Day. Dress Like A Traveller.
lines: Ikat prints, linen layers, canvas tote — pop-up ready.
Global textiles for a city-wide travel culture moment.
Dress like your next destination is already decided.

social_media.headline: Pop-up Hopping. Dressed Like A Globe-Trotter.
lines: POV: walked into World Tourism Day pop-ups in an ikat co-ord with a canvas tote and looked like you'd just returned from somewhere beautiful.
World Tourism Day fit check — global prints, linen textures, cargo details, tote bag ready, dressed like wanderlust is your whole personality.

events.title: That Girl Reset Day
events.genres: Wellness, Self-Care, Pop Culture, Gen Z Cultural Moment
events.description: That Girl Reset Day returns for September — a monthly Gen Z ritual of recommitting to the green smoothie, the 5am walk, the journaling, and the hyper-productive clean-girl aesthetic that refuses to quit even when the monsoon makes staying in bed very tempting.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: That Girl Reset Day September edition — genZScore 8-9. We ran this in March already so the core aesthetic is established — but September in Bengaluru has a distinct character from March that changes the fashion brief meaningfully. September is late monsoon — still wet and warm but beginning to transition toward the pleasant October season. The monsoon context adds a cozy-but-functional layer to the That Girl aesthetic — slightly warmer tones than the March edition, more layering opportunities, and a shift from crisp spring whites toward warm off-whites, sage, and soft earthy neutrals. The That Girl aesthetic itself remains constant — matching seamless athleisure sets, clean girl dressing, linen basics, neutral tones. But the September monsoon makes ribbed knit co-ords and slightly warmer fabrics more relevant than the March version. The productivity reset angle means the fashion splits between morning workout wear and the daytime clean girl transition look. Nail care remains critically relevant — That Girl always has her nails done. The core palette stays neutral and clean but shifts slightly warmer for September — warm whites, camel, sage, dusty mauve, soft brown.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: That Girl Reset Day September is the monsoon-aware version of the March reset — same clean girl energy but with a warmer, slightly cozier palette that suits the late monsoon season. The matching ribbed athleisure set remains the hero category — but shift toward warmer tones this month — camel, dusty mauve, sage, and warm off-white over the crisp spring whites of March. A ribbed camel co-ord or a sage seamless set captures the September That Girl energy perfectly. The knit fabric becomes more relevant in September — a ribbed knit co-ord transitions beautifully from morning walk to café in the Bengaluru September drizzle. Women in a seamless athleisure set in warm neutrals for the morning routine — transitioning to a linen co-ord or oversized cotton button-down over yoga wear for the daytime clean girl look. Men in lightweight jogger sets or a breathable cotton tee with straight-fit chinos in neutral tones — That Girl energy is gender-inclusive in September 2026. A light shrug or zip-up jacket adds a monsoon layer without breaking the clean aesthetic. Dainty gold jewellery — a thin chain, small hoop earrings, a simple ring stack. Caps in neutral camel or sage for the morning walk. Nail care is the beauty signature — a fresh nude or soft-toned manicure is the That Girl September beauty statement. Mobile accessories for the journaling and content creation that defines the reset day. The overall energy is — recommitting to yourself in September with the warmth the season demands.

app.headline: September Reset. That Girl Is Back.
lines: Camel ribbed set, sage co-ord — monsoon That Girl sorted.
New month, same clean girl energy, warmer palette.
Reset day starts with the outfit that makes you feel like her.

social_media.headline: September That Girl Reset. Warmer Now.
lines: POV: September 1st and your camel ribbed co-ord is doing more work than your entire vision board.
That Girl September reset — warm neutrals, ribbed textures, dainty gold, nails done, green smoothie optional but the fit is mandatory.

events.title: Krishna Janmashtami
events.description: Krishna Janmashtami celebrates the birth of Lord Krishna at midnight — marked with fasting, devotional singing, Dahi Handi celebrations, temple decorations, and the most joyful and colorful religious fashion moment dedicated to the most beloved deity in Hindu tradition.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Krishna Janmashtami is one of Hinduism's most joyful and visually vibrant festivals — genZScore 4-7. The fashion energy is uniquely distinctive — Krishna's iconic colors are peacock blue, yellow, and gold — these are the most sacred and catalog-relevant colors of the day. Two distinct fashion contexts: (1) temple and puja dressing — traditional ethnic wear in Krishna's colors, devotional and family-oriented; (2) GenZ Janmashtami celebrations — costume dressing as Radha-Krishna is a hugely popular trend, making ethnic fusion and costume-adjacent ethnic wear very relevant. The Radha-Krishna costume angle is uniquely strong for this festival — yellow and green for Radha, peacock blue and yellow for Krishna. Peacock motifs, lotus prints, and flute-inspired accessories are all catalog-relevant. September in Bengaluru is late monsoon — breathable fabrics essential. Midnight celebrations mean the event spans day and night — the dressing evolves from daytime puja to midnight Dahi Handi celebrations. GenZ crowd dresses with maximum creativity for Janmashtami — it is the most costume-forward Hindu festival after Navratri.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Krishna Janmashtami is the most costume-forward Hindu festival after Navratri — and the most creatively dressed. Peacock blue and yellow are the non-negotiable hero colors — Lord Krishna's iconic palette. Women dressing as Radha in bright yellow or green lehenga choli with gota-patti and mirror-work is the most popular and catalog-relevant Janmashtami look — yellow lehenga with green dupatta or peacock blue blouse. Dressing as Krishna in peacock blue dhoti-kurta with yellow accents and a peacock feather accessory is equally iconic for men and boys. Beyond the costume angle — women in peacock blue ethnic sets with gold mirror-work, or a yellow bandhej lehenga with teal blouse. Men in peacock blue or yellow kurta-pyjama with gold detailing. The peacock motif in fabric prints and jewellery is uniquely relevant for Janmashtami — kalamkari and hand-painted peacock prints are deeply on-brand. Lotus and flute motifs in accessories and surface styling reference Krishna's iconography beautifully. Jewellery is maximalist and gold-heavy — layered necklaces, jhumkas, stacked bangles, kamarbandh, maang tikka. Kids ethnic sets in peacock blue and yellow are the most heartwarming catalog category — little Krishnas and Radhas are the most photographed fashion moment of the festival. The midnight Dahi Handi celebrations call for practical yet festive dressing — breathable fabrics that allow movement. September monsoon means lightweight fabrics — cotton-silk blends and georgette-feel over heavy silks.

app.headline: Peacock Blue And Yellow. Jai Shri Krishna.
lines: Radha yellow or Krishna blue — Janmashtami sorted.
Mirror-work, peacock motifs, and gold jewels for the midnight celebration.
Dress for the most joyful midnight in the Hindu calendar.

social_media.headline: Janmashtami Fits. Peacock Energy. Go.
lines: POV: dressed as Radha in a yellow gota-patti lehenga at Janmashtami and Lord Krishna's playlist started playing.
Janmashtami fit check — peacock blue ethnic set, gold mirror-work, maang tikka, kamarbandh. Jai Shri Krishna. 🦚

events.title: Teacher's Day
events.description: Teacher's Day — celebrated on Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan's birthday — a day of gratitude, appreciation, and celebration for the educators who shape every generation, marked by school events, student performances, and heartfelt gestures.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Teacher's Day is a unique fashion moment with two completely distinct dressing contexts — genZScore 5-7. Context 1: the teachers being celebrated — professional, smart, and elevated workwear that reflects the dignity of their profession. Context 2: the students celebrating — school and college students who often dress up, perform, and sometimes imitate their teachers as part of celebrations. The fashion energy is smart, professional, and respectful with a warm celebratory undercurrent. This is one of the strongest workwear fashion moments in the calendar — blazers, formal shirts, sarees for teachers, smart co-ords. The teacher aesthetic in India has its own fashion language — sarees are the iconic teacher dressing choice, particularly cotton and linen sarees in professional tones. September in Bengaluru is late monsoon — breathable fabrics essential. For students — smart casual with a respectful elevated tone — no casual streetwear. Colors are professional and warm — navy, deep blue, maroon, olive, cream, white, teal, and soft professional tones. The gifting context makes meaningful accessories relevant — watches, pens as gifts implied.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Teacher's Day has two equally valid dressing briefs — the teacher and the student. For teachers — the saree is the most iconic and respected Teacher's Day look in India. A cotton or linen saree in professional deep tones — navy, teal, maroon, or olive — with a minimal zari border and simple gold jewellery is the quintessential Indian teacher aesthetic. Chikankari sarees in cream or white are elegant and breathable for the September monsoon. A well-fitted salwar suit or ethnic set in professional tones is equally appropriate. Men teachers in a crisp formal shirt with tailored trousers, or a clean kurta-pyjama — a blazer or waistcoat elevates the look significantly. Ties and pocket squares are the most teacher-appropriate accessories for men — uniquely relevant today. A watch as both a personal accessory and a gifting reference. For students — smart casual with a respectful elevated tone. Women students in a neat salwar suit, a clean co-ord, or a formal dress — nothing too casual or too party-ready. Men students in a clean formal or semi-formal shirt with trousers. Blazers are the single most versatile Teacher's Day piece for both teachers and students — a structured blazer in navy or charcoal elevates any outfit to the right professional register. September monsoon means breathable cotton and linen for all-day comfort in school or college. The overall energy is — dressed with the respect and pride that education deserves.

app.headline: Teacher's Day. Dress With Respect.
lines: Saree, blazer, or kurta — every teacher deserves their best look.
Professional, warm, and worthy of the most important room.
Dressed for the person who shaped everything you know.

social_media.headline: Best Dressed Teacher. Every Classroom.
lines: POV: wore a teal linen saree to Teacher's Day and every student said you looked like a main character.
Teacher's Day fit check — crisp saree or blazer, minimal gold jewels, professional tones, dressed like knowledge is your whole personality.
